patches.rationale: Directly supported by the fronting intent and source specification purpose statements.
patches.value: Provide a governed ANIP fronting service for Notion so agents can search workspace context, query database context, and prepare page or comment changes through bounded capabilities without receiving raw Notion API, MCP, or unrestricted workspace tools.

patches.rationale: The brief repeatedly states that raw workspace tools should not be exposed and lists the unsafe behaviors that must be denied.
patches.value: Raw Notion workspace operations are powerful but unsafe to expose directly to agents because they can enable unrestricted reads, hidden page access, raw export, arbitrary mutation, and other actions that bypass visibility and approval boundaries.

patches.rationale: These goals are explicitly described across the service boundary, policy semantics, and fronting intent sections.
value: Enable useful Notion knowledge-work operations through a governed agent-facing surface instead of raw workspace tooling.
Keep reads bounded to actor-visible workspace, page, and database scope and return summaries rather than raw exports.
Support safe preparation flows for page creation, page updates, and comments while stopping before mutation until approval is granted.
Make clarification, denial, approval records, and audit part of the operating model.

patches.rationale: These stable task families map directly to the governed capability surface described in the brief.
This is a concise PM-readable synthesis of the policy semantics and fronting intent.
patches.value: The product exposes only bounded Notion capabilities. Reads are limited to actor-visible workspace, page, database, and result scope and return summaries rather than raw exports. Missing required context returns clarification-required. Page creation, page updates, and comments stop at preview and require approval before any mutation. Private page exfiltration, hidden page access, raw export, arbitrary block mutation, workspace administration, and unapproved mutations are denied.

patches.rationale: The source clearly distinguishes read behavior from approval-gated write-adjacent preparation flows.
patches.value: Read operations can proceed within bounded visible scope. Any page creation, page update, or comment action is preview-only and approval-gated before mutation, with approval records and audit expected as part of the service posture.

patches.rationale: The brief provides concrete, reviewable product intent for the core summary fields, making the Product Summary suitable to lock after explicit acceptance.

watchouts: The brief is strong on governance and capability boundaries, but weaker on explicit business KPIs such as time saved, adoption, or compliance targets.

The package uses native Notion API as backend implementation; MCP is explicitly a comparison point, not the agent-facing interface.

items.body: The product must provide an ANIP fronting service for Notion that exposes only bounded capabilities for workspace search, database query, page creation preparation, page update preparation, and comment preparation. Agents must not receive raw Notion API access, raw workspace tools, or MCP as their execution surface.
items.rationale: All sources consistently define the core product boundary as governed fronting over Notion rather than direct tool exposure.
items.title: Expose only governed Notion capabilities to agents

items.body: Workspace search and database query must be read-only, limited to allowed workspace, page, and database boundaries, and return actor-visible summarized context rather than raw exports. Result limits must be supported, and provider-specific options may be allowed only when they remain bounded and do not bypass scope or approval policy.
items.rationale: The source explicitly requires bounded reads, actor-visible summaries, result limits, and denial of raw export or hidden access.
items.title: Keep read operations scoped and summary-only

items.body: Page creation, page updates, and comments must be handled as preparation flows that generate previews only. The system must not create pages, apply updates, or post comments as part of these capabilities. Any actual mutation must remain approval-gated beyond the preview step.
items.rationale: The structured operations and fronting intent both define these capabilities as prepare-only and approval-gated before mutation.
items.title: Stop page and comment changes at preview before mutation

items.body: The product must require explicit inputs for governed operations, including workspace scope for workspace search, database ID for database query, parent ID for page creation preparation, page ID for page update preparation and comment preparation, and the relevant query or summary fields for each flow. When required page, database, parent, workspace scope, or query context is missing or ambiguous, the system must return clarification-required rather than guessing.
items.rationale: The backend evidence includes explicit required inputs and clarification hints, and the fronting intent makes clarification-required a policy behavior.
items.title: Require explicit identifiers and return clarification-required when context is missing

items.body: The product must deny requests that would expose private pages, hidden pages, raw workspace export, workspace administration, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, or any unapproved mutation. Backend options may support bounded provider controls such as selected properties, page size, or safe filter fragments, but must not expand access or bypass governance.
items.rationale: The source specification and fronting intent both enumerate denied behaviors and constrain backend options to safe bounded controls.
items.title: Deny unsafe or out-of-bound Notion actions

items.body: The service must operate with actor-visible scope controls, approval records for change flows, and auditability sufficient for governance review. The deployment posture should support centralized enforcement of allowlists, visibility boundaries, and reviewable outcomes for both bounded reads and denied or approval-stopped actions.
items.rationale: The service boundary description explicitly mentions allowlists, actor-visible scope, approval records, and audit, but the exact audit artifact shape is not yet defined.
items.title: Preserve governance visibility through scope, approvals, and audit

questions_for_user: Should approval-gated previews include a named approver workflow, or only stop at a review boundary for now?
Do you want actor-visible summaries to follow a standard response shape across workspace search and database query?

# Source 2: Notion governed fronting source specification
Kind: Fronting intent
Filename: source-spec.md

# Notion Governed Fronting Showcase Source Specification

This source document models a realistic fronting use case: Notion API exists downstream, but agents should only see governed workspace capabilities.

## Purpose

Demonstrate that ANIP can govern knowledge-work operations without exposing raw workspace read/write tools directly to agents.

The ANIP contract owns the workspace behavior. The Notion API is the backend for this showcase. Notion MCP may be useful as a comparison point for raw tool exposure, but this package does not use MCP as the ANIP execution interface.

## Service Boundary

- Service ID: `notion-governance-service`
- Service name: Notion Governance Service
- Primary backend: native Notion API adapter
- Deployment posture: centralized ANIP fronting service with workspace/page allowlists, actor-visible scope, approval records, and audit.

## Backend Evidence

Native Notion API supply:

- Workspace search through `/search`.
- Database query through `/databases/{database_id}/query`.
- Page creation through `/pages`.
- Page metadata update through `/pages/{page_id}` and content block append/update through block APIs.
- Comments through `/comments`.

MCP comparison:

- Notion MCP may expose search, database query, page, block, and comment operations.
- This showcase intentionally does not bind ANIP capabilities to Notion MCP tools. The agent-facing surface remains ANIP capabilities backed by bounded Notion API operations.

## Governed Capability Surface

| Capability | User intent | Required inputs | Optional governed inputs | Outcome posture |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `notion.workspace.search_context` | Search bounded pages and databases. | `workspace_scope`, `query` | `limit`, `object_type`, `backend_options` | Read-only. Actor-visible summaries only. |
| `notion.database.query_context` | Query a bounded database view. | `database_id` | `filter`, `sort`, `limit`, `backend_options` | Read-only. Returns summarized context, not raw export. |
| `notion.page.create.prepare` | Prepare a page under an explicit parent. | `parent_id`, `title`, `content_summary` | `template_hint`, `backend_options` | Preview-only. Requires approval before creation. |
| `notion.page.update.prepare` | Prepare a page update. | `page_id`, `change_summary` | `content_patch`, `backend_options` | Preview-only. Requires approval before applying. |
| `notion.comment.prepare` | Prepare a page comment. | `page_id`, `comment_purpose`, `context` | `backend_options` | Preview-only. Requires approval before posting. |

`backend_options` is limited to bounded provider controls such as selected properties, page size, or safe filter fragments. It must not allow arbitrary block mutation, workspace-wide export, parent override, or hidden page access.

## Policy Semantics

- Reads are bounded by actor-visible workspace, page, database, and result limits.
- Missing page, database, parent, or query context returns `clarification_required`.
- Page creation, page updates, and comments are preview/approval flows.
- Private page exfiltration, raw workspace export, workspace admin actions, and unapproved mutations are denied.
- Native API is the execution binding for this package. MCP can be used as a comparison surface, but is not required for ANIP.

## Why ANIP Helps

Notion API access provides powerful workspace operations. ANIP makes the safe boundary explicit: search can be read-only and bounded, while page and comment changes stop at preview or approval.

# Source 3: Notion Fronting Showcase 0.2.0 fronting intent
Kind: Fronting intent
Filename: notion-fronting-intent.md

# Notion Fronting Showcase 0.2.0 Fronting Intent

Build a governed ANIP fronting service for Notion. Agents should not receive raw Notion API, MCP, or workspace tools. They should receive bounded capabilities for workspace search, database query, page creation preparation, page update preparation, and comment preparation. Reads must be scoped to allowed workspace/page/database boundaries and return actor-visible summaries, not raw exports. Page creation, page updates, and comments must be preview-only and approval-gated before mutation. Missing page, database, parent, workspace scope, or query inputs should return clarification-required. Private page exfiltration, arbitrary block mutation, workspace administration, hidden page access, raw export, and unapproved mutation must be denied. Native Notion API is the backend implementation path.

## Next review step
Add integration evidence from a real source before contract generation. Use uploaded OpenAPI, GraphQL, MCP schema, API docs, permission matrices, live discovery, or manual operation entry. Studio must not invent backend operations from the project name or domain.

data.policy_summary: Notion access is governed through bounded ANIP capabilities only. Workspace search and database query return actor-visible summaries within declared scope and result limits. Page creation, page updates, and comments are preview-only and stop for approval before any mutation. Missing workspace, page, parent, database, or query context returns clarification-required. Raw workspace export, hidden page access, workspace administration, arbitrary block mutation, private page exfiltration, and unapproved mutation are denied.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded workspace search within declared workspace scope and return actor-visible summaries only, not raw workspace tools or exports.
rules.notes: Matches the governed search capability and read-only bounded result posture.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded database query against an explicit database and return summarized context rather than raw export.
rules.notes: Filters, sorting, and backend options remain bounded by scope and policy.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of a page creation preview under an explicit parent, then stop for approval before creation.
rules.notes: No direct page creation is allowed from the governed capability surface.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of a page update preview for an explicit page, then stop for approval before applying changes.
rules.notes: No direct page update is allowed from the governed capability surface.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of a page comment preview from supplied context, then stop for approval before posting.
rules.notes: No direct comment posting is allowed from the governed capability surface.

rules.governed_outcome: Stop and request clarification when workspace scope, query, page id, parent id, or database id is missing or ambiguous.
rules.notes: Applies across search, query, and preparation flows.

rules.governed_outcome: Deny requests for hidden page access, private page exfiltration, raw workspace export, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, or workspace-wide unrestricted access.
rules.notes: Preserves actor-visible scope and bounded provider controls.

rules.governed_outcome: Deny direct mutation attempts when approval has not been granted for page creation, page update, or comment posting.
rules.notes: Separates preview preparation from actual mutation authority.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow review and approval decision handling for prepared page creation, page update, and comment previews.
rules.notes: This rule assumes the approver actor is intended to participate directly in approval flow.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow governance review of approval records, scope enforcement posture, and audit-relevant outcomes for the governed Notion fronting service.
rules.notes: This rule assumes governance review and audit visibility are intended for this actor.

data.approval_posture_summary: Read operations can proceed within bounded visible scope. Any page creation, page update, or comment action is preview-only and approval-gated before mutation, with approval records and audit expected as part of the service posture.
business_goals: Enable useful Notion knowledge-work operations through a governed agent-facing surface instead of raw workspace tooling.
Keep reads bounded to actor-visible workspace, page, and database scope and return summaries rather than raw exports.
Support safe preparation flows for page creation, page updates, and comments while stopping before mutation until approval is granted.
Make clarification, denial, approval records, and audit part of the operating model.
data.business_problem: Raw Notion workspace operations are powerful but unsafe to expose directly to agents because they can enable unrestricted reads, hidden page access, raw export, arbitrary mutation, and other actions that bypass visibility and approval boundaries.
data.governed_behavior_summary: The product exposes only bounded Notion capabilities. Reads are limited to actor-visible workspace, page, database, and result scope and return summaries rather than raw exports. Missing required context returns clarification-required. Page creation, page updates, and comments stop at preview and require approval before any mutation. Private page exfiltration, hidden page access, raw export, arbitrary block mutation, workspace administration, and unapproved mutations are denied.
data.product_purpose: Provide a governed ANIP fronting service for Notion so agents can search workspace context, query database context, and prepare page or comment changes through bounded capabilities without receiving raw Notion API, MCP, or unrestricted workspace tools.

items.body: Allow runtime invocation only for the locked capability inventory: notion.workspace.search_context, notion.database.query_context, notion.page.create.prepare, notion.page.update.prepare, and notion.comment.prepare. Deny any attempt to expose raw Notion API access, raw workspace tools, MCP-style execution surfaces, workspace administration, raw export behavior, hidden page access, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, or any other out-of-scope action through the same service boundary.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly limits the product to governed fronting capabilities and explicitly denies raw workspace access and unsafe actions.
items.title: Bind runtime access to the governed Notion capability surface only

items.body: For each governed capability, require explicit target identifiers and minimum request context before execution. Return clarification_required when workspace_scope or query is missing for workspace search, database_id is missing for database query, parent_id is missing for page creation preparation, page_id is missing for page update preparation or comment preparation, or when required summaries such as title, content_summary, change_summary, comment_purpose, or context are missing or ambiguous. Do not infer targets, default to likely pages or databases, or continue with partial context.
items.rationale: The baseline and operation evidence both require explicit inputs and state that the service must stop for clarification rather than guess.
items.title: Make missing explicit identifiers a clarification-required stop

items.body: For notion.workspace.search_context and notion.database.query_context, enforce read-only execution, allowed workspace or database boundaries, actor-visible results, and summarized output shaping. Permit result limits and bounded provider controls only when they remain within declared scope and do not produce raw exports, unrestricted retrieval, or hidden-content exposure. Runtime responses should preserve the distinction between allowed bounded reads and denied attempts to expand access.
items.rationale: The baseline requires summary-only bounded reads with result limits and safe provider-specific options, while denying raw export and hidden access.
items.title: Bind read operations to bounded, summary-only outputs

items.body: For notion.page.create.prepare, notion.page.update.prepare, and notion.comment.prepare, allow only preparation behavior that returns a preview artifact and stops before mutation. The runtime contract should mark these outcomes as approval-gated and must not create pages, apply updates, or post comments as part of these capabilities. Preserve explicit target scope during preview generation and deny any provider option that attempts to convert preparation into execution or bypass the approval boundary.
items.rationale: The baseline repeatedly states that page creation, page update, and comment flows are preparation-only and must stop before mutation pending approval.
items.title: Bind write-adjacent capabilities to preview-only outcomes with approval stop

items.body: Return deny for requests that seek raw workspace export, private or hidden page access, workspace administration, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, unapproved mutation, or any backend option that expands access beyond the governed surface. Denial responses should preserve safe-stop visibility and be reviewable without leaking prohibited content. Runtime policy should distinguish deny from clarification_required and from approval_required so prohibited actions are not treated as incomplete requests.
items.rationale: The denial list is explicit in the baseline scenarios and requirements, and the product intent calls for visible, reviewable safe denials.
items.title: Make denial behavior explicit for prohibited Notion requests

items.body: Require runtime policy enforcement to emit durable, searchable audit records for allowed reads, clarification stops, denials, and approval-stopped preview flows. Include signed trust posture and lineage fields sufficient for governance review, including task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id when present. The runtime binding should ensure that policy decisions and bounded outcomes remain reviewable across the public HTTP service surface.
items.rationale: The locked baseline requires signed trust mode, durable searchable audit, and explicit lineage fields for governance visibility.
items.title: Bind all governed outcomes to signed, durable audit lineage

questions_for_user: Should approval-required preview outputs include a named approval authority now, or stay abstract until a later governance pass?
Do you want explicit numeric max limits for search and database query results in this policy layer, or should result limiting remain implementation-defined?
Should backend_options be fully omitted from agent-facing contracts unless explicitly requested, or merely allowed but tightly validated?
envelope.summary: Candidate runtime policy binding blocks for the Notion fronting service, grounded in the locked baseline. Focus is explicit capability gating, clarification stops, preview-only write posture, bounded provider options, and auditable denial behavior.
envelope.title: Runtime Policy Binding Proposal: Notion Fronting Showcase 0.2.0
watchouts: The baseline supports preview-only preparation for page creation, page update, and comment flows; it does not support executing the final mutation.
Do not broaden the runtime surface beyond the locked capability inventory.
Clarification-required is a first-class governed outcome and should not be collapsed into denial.
Bounded provider controls are allowed only when they do not expand scope, reveal hidden content, override parent targeting, or bypass approval policy.
Read capabilities must return summarized, actor-visible context rather than raw exports or raw workspace access.

next_steps: Review and accept or edit the candidate binding blocks individually.

items.body: Define a shared backend binding for the Notion Governance service that uses backend_kind `native_api` and connection_ref `notion_fronting` for all canonical capabilities. Treat this as the only downstream integration surface for governed Notion access in this service.
items.rationale: The source backend evidence names a single service, backend kind, and connection_ref across all operations.
items.title: Bind the Notion Governance service to the shared Notion fronting connection

items.body: Bind capability `notion.workspace.search_context` to path `/notion/workspace_search_context` with raw operation reference `notion.api.workspace_search_context`. Require explicit `workspace_scope` and `query`; allow optional `limit` and bounded `backend_options`. Mark the operation read-only and return bounded workspace results rather than raw exports.
items.rationale: The locked baseline and backend operation evidence align on explicit inputs, read-only behavior, and summary-only workspace search.
items.title: Bind workspace search to the bounded search endpoint and explicit input contract

items.body: Bind capability `notion.database.query_context` to path `/notion/database_query_context` with raw operation reference `notion.api.database_query_context`. Require explicit `database_id`; allow optional `filter`, `limit`, and bounded `backend_options`. Mark the operation read-only and shape outputs as summarized rows from an allowed database view.
items.rationale: The source evidence provides a direct endpoint mapping and the baseline requires bounded summarized database context.
items.title: Bind database query to the bounded database context endpoint

items.body: Bind capability `notion.page.create.prepare` to path `/notion/page_create_prepare` with raw operation reference `notion.api.page_create_prepare`. Require explicit `parent_id`, `title`, and `content_summary`; allow optional bounded `backend_options`. Mark the operation as write-adjacent only, returning a page creation preview and stopping before any page creation mutation.
items.rationale: Both the scenario baseline and backend evidence explicitly define this as a preparation flow under an explicit parent with no direct mutation.
items.title: Bind page creation preparation to a preview-only endpoint with approval stop

items.body: Bind capability `notion.page.update.prepare` to path `/notion/page_update_prepare` with raw operation reference `notion.api.page_update_prepare`. Require explicit `page_id` and `change_summary`; allow optional `content_patch` and bounded `backend_options`. Mark the operation as write-adjacent only and return an update preview without applying page changes.
items.rationale: The locked scenarios and operation evidence both require explicit page targeting and preview-only update preparation.
items.title: Bind page update preparation to a preview-only endpoint without apply behavior

items.body: Bind capability `notion.comment.prepare` to path `/notion/comment_prepare` with raw operation reference `notion.api.comment_prepare`. Require explicit `page_id`, `comment_purpose`, and `context`; allow optional bounded `backend_options`. Mark the operation as write-adjacent only and return a comment preview without posting.
items.rationale: The source evidence directly specifies the endpoint and the baseline requires comment preparation to stop before posting.
items.title: Bind comment preparation to a preview-only endpoint tied to an explicit page

items.body: Define shared backend outcome handling across all five capabilities so missing or ambiguous required identifiers produce `clarification-required`, while prohibited requests produce explicit denial outcomes. Apply this to missing `workspace_scope`, `query`, `database_id`, `parent_id`, `page_id`, and other required preparation fields, and to prohibited requests such as raw export, hidden page access, admin actions, parent override, arbitrary block mutation, or approval bypass.
items.rationale: The locked baseline makes clarification-required and denial core governed behaviors rather than incidental error handling.
items.title: Add explicit backend outcome bindings for clarification-required and denial

items.body: Define backend binding rules that treat `backend_options` as an optional bounded object for safe provider controls such as selected properties, page size, or safe filter fragments. Reject or strip any option that expands scope, changes parent targeting, exposes hidden content, enables arbitrary mutation, or bypasses approval policy.
items.rationale: The operation evidence repeatedly allows backend_options only within bounded policy limits.
items.title: Constrain backend_options to bounded provider controls only

items.body: Require every backend invocation and governed outcome to emit durable, searchable audit records and propagate lineage fields including `task_id`, `invocation_id`, `client_reference_id`, and `parent_invocation_id`. Include both successful bounded reads and safe-stop outcomes such as denial and approval-stopped previews.
items.rationale: The locked baseline explicitly requires durable searchable audit and lineage visibility, though exact storage mechanics are not yet specified.
items.title: Bind all backend calls to durable audit and lineage propagation

items.body: Define backend invocation bindings so each call is executed under signed trust mode with purpose binding, scoped authority, and delegation-token support. The binding should require actor-visible scope enforcement before downstream execution and should prevent capability use outside the declared governed purpose.
items.rationale: The requirements baseline specifies signed trust, purpose binding, scoped authority, and delegation tokens, but does not yet define the exact token format.
items.title: Make signed, purpose-bound, scoped authority checks part of backend invocation binding

questions_for_user: Should all five capabilities bind through the single documented connection_ref `notion_fronting`, or do you expect environment-specific connection aliases?
Do you want backend bindings to pin exact HTTP methods and request/response schemas now, or keep this pass at endpoint-and-policy binding level only?
Should approval-stop preview outputs be bound to a separate approval record store now, or only require audit emission in this pass?
Do you want explicit allowlist sources defined for workspace_scope, database_id, parent_id, and page_id resolution, or should bindings only state that allowlist enforcement is required?
envelope.summary: Candidate backend-binding blocks for the single Notion Governance service, grounded in the locked capability inventory and the supplied Notion backend operation evidence. These blocks focus on explicit endpoint, connection, policy, and audit bindings needed for implementation review.
envelope.title: Backend Binding Proposal: Notion Fronting Showcase 0.2.0
watchouts: The locked baseline permits only the five canonical capabilities; backend bindings must not introduce raw Notion API exposure, export endpoints, admin actions, or direct mutation paths.
Write-adjacent capabilities are preview-only bindings. They must stop before create, update, or comment posting and must not imply execution bindings for final mutation.
Optional backend_options are bounded provider controls only and must not be treated as a generic pass-through to unrestricted Notion parameters.
Clarification-required is a first-class governed outcome and should be represented in bindings for missing or ambiguous required identifiers.
Audit and lineage fields are required by baseline intent; bindings should preserve task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id across calls.

next_steps: Review and accept or edit the candidate verification blocks.
Map each accepted block to concrete test suites, runtime assertions, and audit evidence requirements.
Add scenario coverage traces for the seven locked baseline scenarios across positive, clarification, and denial paths.

items.body: For notion.workspace.search_context and notion.database.query_context, verify that execution requires explicit required identifiers and query inputs, respects declared scope boundaries, enforces result limits, and returns actor-visible summarized results rather than raw exports or raw workspace access. Verification should include positive cases for allowed bounded reads and negative cases proving hidden pages, private page exfiltration, unrestricted export, and workspace administration are denied.
items.rationale: The locked baseline makes bounded read behavior central: workspace search and database query are read-only, scoped, limited, and summary-only, with explicit denial of raw export and hidden access.
items.title: Verify governed read capabilities stay bounded, explicit, and summary-only

items.body: For all five locked capabilities, verify that missing or ambiguous required inputs produce a clarification-required outcome instead of guessed targets, hidden defaults, or partial execution. Coverage should include workspace_scope and query for search, database_id for database query, parent_id for page creation preparation, and page_id for page update and comment preparation, plus required descriptive fields such as title, content_summary, change_summary, comment_purpose, and context. Verification should confirm that only the minimum missing information is requested and that execution does not proceed until supplied.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires clarification-required when required page, database, parent, workspace scope, or query context is missing or ambiguous, and the operation evidence defines those required inputs concretely.
items.title: Verify clarification-required behavior for missing or ambiguous Notion context

items.body: For notion.page.create.prepare, notion.page.update.prepare, and notion.comment.prepare, verify that the service produces preview artifacts only and never performs the underlying Notion mutation as part of the capability. Tests should confirm explicit target requirements, visible approval-stop outcomes, and absence of created pages, applied updates, or posted comments. Verification should also confirm that parent override, arbitrary block mutation, and other unapproved write paths are unavailable through normal inputs or backend_options.
items.rationale: The locked scenarios and requirements state that page creation, updates, and comments are preparation flows only and must stop at preview before any mutation occurs.
items.title: Verify page creation, page update, and comment flows are preview-only and approval-stopped

items.body: Define negative verification coverage that proves the governed Notion surface denies unsupported actions, including raw workspace export, hidden or private page access, workspace administration, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, and any unapproved mutation attempt. Verification should confirm that denial outcomes are explicit, reviewable, and narrower than backend capability possibilities, and that denied requests do not leak raw data or execute partial side effects.
items.rationale: A dedicated locked scenario covers denial of raw export, hidden pages, and unapproved mutation, and the requirements explicitly call out these unsafe actions as denied.
items.title: Verify explicit denial behavior for prohibited and out-of-bound requests

items.body: Where backend_options are accepted, verify they are optional, explicit-only, and constrained to safe provider controls such as selected properties, page size, or safe filter fragments. Negative tests should prove backend_options cannot expand workspace or database scope, expose hidden content, override explicit parent or page targets, enable arbitrary mutation, or bypass approval boundaries. Verification should also confirm the service does not ask for backend_options unless provider-specific controls are actually needed.
items.rationale: Both the baseline and backend operation evidence allow provider-specific controls only when bounded and explicitly forbid bypassing scope or approval policy.
items.title: Verify backend options remain bounded and cannot bypass governance

items.body: Verify that bounded reads, clarification-required outcomes, denials, and approval-stopped preview flows all produce durable, searchable governance evidence with lineage sufficient for review. At minimum, verification should confirm presence of task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id where applicable, along with reviewable outcome classification and actor-visible scope context. The goal is to prove centralized enforcement and reviewability rather than only functional success.
items.rationale: The locked baseline requires durable searchable audit, lineage identifiers, and governance visibility through scope, approvals, and reviewable outcomes, but exact record shape is not fully specified here.
items.title: Verify governance visibility through durable audit and lineage evidence

items.body: Establish a verification matrix that traces implementation evidence to the seven locked scenarios: bounded workspace search, bounded database query, page creation preparation, page update preparation, comment preparation, clarification-required handling, and denial of prohibited requests. Each scenario should have at least one positive or expected-governed path and one guard-path check where relevant, so verification demonstrates contract stability across the full governed surface rather than isolated endpoint tests.
items.rationale: The locked baseline already defines the scenario pack that expresses intended runtime behavior, so verification should trace directly to that set.
items.title: Verify coverage against the locked scenario pack

questions_for_user: Should verification require concrete audit record fields for approval-stopped and denied outcomes, or is outcome presence sufficient in this pass?
Do you want explicit negative-test coverage for every capability's backend_options handling, or only for representative denial cases?
Should result summarization quality be verified structurally only, or also against content-level examples?
envelope.summary: Candidate developer-facing verification expectation blocks derived from the locked Notion fronting baseline, scenario set, and backend operation evidence. Focus is on proving governed reads, clarification stops, preview-only write-adjacent flows, denial behavior, and governance visibility without expanding scope.
envelope.title: Verification Expectations Proposal: Notion Fronting Showcase 0.2.0
watchouts: These are review candidates only and should be accepted or edited before persistence.
Verification should preserve the locked capability inventory and not introduce direct mutation or raw Notion access paths.
The baseline requires clarification-required on missing required context; verification should not allow silent defaults or inferred targets.
Preview flows must stop before mutation, so tests that accidentally perform create, update, or comment posting would violate product intent.
Provider-specific options are allowed only when bounded; verification should ensure they cannot widen scope, override parent/page targets, or bypass approval policy.

accepted_payload.body: Allow runtime invocation only for the locked capability inventory: notion.workspace.search_context, notion.database.query_context, notion.page.create.prepare, notion.page.update.prepare, and notion.comment.prepare. Deny any attempt to expose raw Notion API access, raw workspace tools, MCP-style execution surfaces, workspace administration, raw export behavior, hidden page access, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, or any other out-of-scope action through the same service boundary.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline explicitly limits the product to governed fronting capabilities and explicitly denies raw workspace access and unsafe actions.
accepted_payload.title: Bind runtime access to the governed Notion capability surface only

accepted_payload.body: For each governed capability, require explicit target identifiers and minimum request context before execution. Return clarification_required when workspace_scope or query is missing for workspace search, database_id is missing for database query, parent_id is missing for page creation preparation, page_id is missing for page update preparation or comment preparation, or when required summaries such as title, content_summary, change_summary, comment_purpose, or context are missing or ambiguous. Do not infer targets, default to likely pages or databases, or continue with partial context.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline and operation evidence both require explicit inputs and state that the service must stop for clarification rather than guess.
accepted_payload.title: Make missing explicit identifiers a clarification-required stop

accepted_payload.body: For notion.workspace.search_context and notion.database.query_context, enforce read-only execution, allowed workspace or database boundaries, actor-visible results, and summarized output shaping. Permit result limits and bounded provider controls only when they remain within declared scope and do not produce raw exports, unrestricted retrieval, or hidden-content exposure. Runtime responses should preserve the distinction between allowed bounded reads and denied attempts to expand access.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires summary-only bounded reads with result limits and safe provider-specific options, while denying raw export and hidden access.
accepted_payload.title: Bind read operations to bounded, summary-only outputs

accepted_payload.body: For notion.page.create.prepare, notion.page.update.prepare, and notion.comment.prepare, allow only preparation behavior that returns a preview artifact and stops before mutation. The runtime contract should mark these outcomes as approval-gated and must not create pages, apply updates, or post comments as part of these capabilities. Preserve explicit target scope during preview generation and deny any provider option that attempts to convert preparation into execution or bypass the approval boundary.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline repeatedly states that page creation, page update, and comment flows are preparation-only and must stop before mutation pending approval.
accepted_payload.title: Bind write-adjacent capabilities to preview-only outcomes with approval stop

accepted_payload.body: Return deny for requests that seek raw workspace export, private or hidden page access, workspace administration, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, unapproved mutation, or any backend option that expands access beyond the governed surface. Denial responses should preserve safe-stop visibility and be reviewable without leaking prohibited content. Runtime policy should distinguish deny from clarification_required and from approval_required so prohibited actions are not treated as incomplete requests.
accepted_payload.rationale: The denial list is explicit in the baseline scenarios and requirements, and the product intent calls for visible, reviewable safe denials.
accepted_payload.title: Make denial behavior explicit for prohibited Notion requests

accepted_payload.body: Require runtime policy enforcement to emit durable, searchable audit records for allowed reads, clarification stops, denials, and approval-stopped preview flows. Include signed trust posture and lineage fields sufficient for governance review, including task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id when present. The runtime binding should ensure that policy decisions and bounded outcomes remain reviewable across the public HTTP service surface.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline requires signed trust mode, durable searchable audit, and explicit lineage fields for governance visibility.
accepted_payload.title: Bind all governed outcomes to signed, durable audit lineage

accepted_payload.body: Define a shared backend binding for the Notion Governance service that uses backend_kind `native_api` and connection_ref `notion_fronting` for all canonical capabilities. Treat this as the only downstream integration surface for governed Notion access in this service.
accepted_payload.rationale: The source backend evidence names a single service, backend kind, and connection_ref across all operations.
accepted_payload.title: Bind the Notion Governance service to the shared Notion fronting connection

accepted_payload.body: Bind capability `notion.workspace.search_context` to path `/notion/workspace_search_context` with raw operation reference `notion.api.workspace_search_context`. Require explicit `workspace_scope` and `query`; allow optional `limit` and bounded `backend_options`. Mark the operation read-only and return bounded workspace results rather than raw exports.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline and backend operation evidence align on explicit inputs, read-only behavior, and summary-only workspace search.
accepted_payload.title: Bind workspace search to the bounded search endpoint and explicit input contract

accepted_payload.body: Bind capability `notion.database.query_context` to path `/notion/database_query_context` with raw operation reference `notion.api.database_query_context`. Require explicit `database_id`; allow optional `filter`, `limit`, and bounded `backend_options`. Mark the operation read-only and shape outputs as summarized rows from an allowed database view.
accepted_payload.rationale: The source evidence provides a direct endpoint mapping and the baseline requires bounded summarized database context.
accepted_payload.title: Bind database query to the bounded database context endpoint

accepted_payload.body: Bind capability `notion.page.create.prepare` to path `/notion/page_create_prepare` with raw operation reference `notion.api.page_create_prepare`. Require explicit `parent_id`, `title`, and `content_summary`; allow optional bounded `backend_options`. Mark the operation as write-adjacent only, returning a page creation preview and stopping before any page creation mutation.
accepted_payload.rationale: Both the scenario baseline and backend evidence explicitly define this as a preparation flow under an explicit parent with no direct mutation.
accepted_payload.title: Bind page creation preparation to a preview-only endpoint with approval stop

accepted_payload.body: Bind capability `notion.page.update.prepare` to path `/notion/page_update_prepare` with raw operation reference `notion.api.page_update_prepare`. Require explicit `page_id` and `change_summary`; allow optional `content_patch` and bounded `backend_options`. Mark the operation as write-adjacent only and return an update preview without applying page changes.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked scenarios and operation evidence both require explicit page targeting and preview-only update preparation.
accepted_payload.title: Bind page update preparation to a preview-only endpoint without apply behavior

accepted_payload.body: Bind capability `notion.comment.prepare` to path `/notion/comment_prepare` with raw operation reference `notion.api.comment_prepare`. Require explicit `page_id`, `comment_purpose`, and `context`; allow optional bounded `backend_options`. Mark the operation as write-adjacent only and return a comment preview without posting.
accepted_payload.rationale: The source evidence directly specifies the endpoint and the baseline requires comment preparation to stop before posting.
accepted_payload.title: Bind comment preparation to a preview-only endpoint tied to an explicit page

accepted_payload.body: Define shared backend outcome handling across all five capabilities so missing or ambiguous required identifiers produce `clarification-required`, while prohibited requests produce explicit denial outcomes. Apply this to missing `workspace_scope`, `query`, `database_id`, `parent_id`, `page_id`, and other required preparation fields, and to prohibited requests such as raw export, hidden page access, admin actions, parent override, arbitrary block mutation, or approval bypass.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline makes clarification-required and denial core governed behaviors rather than incidental error handling.
accepted_payload.title: Add explicit backend outcome bindings for clarification-required and denial

accepted_payload.body: Define backend binding rules that treat `backend_options` as an optional bounded object for safe provider controls such as selected properties, page size, or safe filter fragments. Reject or strip any option that expands scope, changes parent targeting, exposes hidden content, enables arbitrary mutation, or bypasses approval policy.
accepted_payload.rationale: The operation evidence repeatedly allows backend_options only within bounded policy limits.
accepted_payload.title: Constrain backend_options to bounded provider controls only

accepted_payload.body: Require every backend invocation and governed outcome to emit durable, searchable audit records and propagate lineage fields including `task_id`, `invocation_id`, `client_reference_id`, and `parent_invocation_id`. Include both successful bounded reads and safe-stop outcomes such as denial and approval-stopped previews.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline explicitly requires durable searchable audit and lineage visibility, though exact storage mechanics are not yet specified.
accepted_payload.title: Bind all backend calls to durable audit and lineage propagation

accepted_payload.body: Define backend invocation bindings so each call is executed under signed trust mode with purpose binding, scoped authority, and delegation-token support. The binding should require actor-visible scope enforcement before downstream execution and should prevent capability use outside the declared governed purpose.
accepted_payload.rationale: The requirements baseline specifies signed trust, purpose binding, scoped authority, and delegation tokens, but does not yet define the exact token format.
accepted_payload.title: Make signed, purpose-bound, scoped authority checks part of backend invocation binding

accepted_payload.body: For notion.workspace.search_context and notion.database.query_context, verify that execution requires explicit required identifiers and query inputs, respects declared scope boundaries, enforces result limits, and returns actor-visible summarized results rather than raw exports or raw workspace access. Verification should include positive cases for allowed bounded reads and negative cases proving hidden pages, private page exfiltration, unrestricted export, and workspace administration are denied.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline makes bounded read behavior central: workspace search and database query are read-only, scoped, limited, and summary-only, with explicit denial of raw export and hidden access.
accepted_payload.title: Verify governed read capabilities stay bounded, explicit, and summary-only

accepted_payload.body: For all five locked capabilities, verify that missing or ambiguous required inputs produce a clarification-required outcome instead of guessed targets, hidden defaults, or partial execution. Coverage should include workspace_scope and query for search, database_id for database query, parent_id for page creation preparation, and page_id for page update and comment preparation, plus required descriptive fields such as title, content_summary, change_summary, comment_purpose, and context. Verification should confirm that only the minimum missing information is requested and that execution does not proceed until supplied.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires clarification-required when required page, database, parent, workspace scope, or query context is missing or ambiguous, and the operation evidence defines those required inputs concretely.
accepted_payload.title: Verify clarification-required behavior for missing or ambiguous Notion context

accepted_payload.body: For notion.page.create.prepare, notion.page.update.prepare, and notion.comment.prepare, verify that the service produces preview artifacts only and never performs the underlying Notion mutation as part of the capability. Tests should confirm explicit target requirements, visible approval-stop outcomes, and absence of created pages, applied updates, or posted comments. Verification should also confirm that parent override, arbitrary block mutation, and other unapproved write paths are unavailable through normal inputs or backend_options.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked scenarios and requirements state that page creation, updates, and comments are preparation flows only and must stop at preview before any mutation occurs.
accepted_payload.title: Verify page creation, page update, and comment flows are preview-only and approval-stopped

accepted_payload.body: Define negative verification coverage that proves the governed Notion surface denies unsupported actions, including raw workspace export, hidden or private page access, workspace administration, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, and any unapproved mutation attempt. Verification should confirm that denial outcomes are explicit, reviewable, and narrower than backend capability possibilities, and that denied requests do not leak raw data or execute partial side effects.
accepted_payload.rationale: A dedicated locked scenario covers denial of raw export, hidden pages, and unapproved mutation, and the requirements explicitly call out these unsafe actions as denied.
accepted_payload.title: Verify explicit denial behavior for prohibited and out-of-bound requests

accepted_payload.body: Where backend_options are accepted, verify they are optional, explicit-only, and constrained to safe provider controls such as selected properties, page size, or safe filter fragments. Negative tests should prove backend_options cannot expand workspace or database scope, expose hidden content, override explicit parent or page targets, enable arbitrary mutation, or bypass approval boundaries. Verification should also confirm the service does not ask for backend_options unless provider-specific controls are actually needed.
accepted_payload.rationale: Both the baseline and backend operation evidence allow provider-specific controls only when bounded and explicitly forbid bypassing scope or approval policy.
accepted_payload.title: Verify backend options remain bounded and cannot bypass governance

accepted_payload.body: Verify that bounded reads, clarification-required outcomes, denials, and approval-stopped preview flows all produce durable, searchable governance evidence with lineage sufficient for review. At minimum, verification should confirm presence of task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id where applicable, along with reviewable outcome classification and actor-visible scope context. The goal is to prove centralized enforcement and reviewability rather than only functional success.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline requires durable searchable audit, lineage identifiers, and governance visibility through scope, approvals, and reviewable outcomes, but exact record shape is not fully specified here.
accepted_payload.title: Verify governance visibility through durable audit and lineage evidence

accepted_payload.body: Establish a verification matrix that traces implementation evidence to the seven locked scenarios: bounded workspace search, bounded database query, page creation preparation, page update preparation, comment preparation, clarification-required handling, and denial of prohibited requests. Each scenario should have at least one positive or expected-governed path and one guard-path check where relevant, so verification demonstrates contract stability across the full governed surface rather than isolated endpoint tests.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline already defines the scenario pack that expresses intended runtime behavior, so verification should trace directly to that set.
accepted_payload.title: Verify coverage against the locked scenario pack

probes.rationale: Declared business effects should make package boundaries testable before paid LLM benchmark runs.

probes.rationale: Compound requests that include approval-preview behavior must select the governed boundary capability instead of a harmless read-only capability.

data.developer_note: Developer Design covers the locked Notion fronting baseline with five governed capabilities, native Notion backend mappings, approval-gated write-adjacent preparation flows, bounded read behavior, denial boundaries, and audit/lineage expectations. Coverage is complete for the current baseline.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded workspace search and bounded database query. Can prepare page creation, page update, and comment previews when explicit parent, page, database, workspace, or query context is supplied. Should be asked for clarification when required context is missing or ambiguous.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: May request preview-only page creation, page update, and comment preparation, but should not be treated as having authority to apply or post mutations unless separately approved.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business user who interacts with the governed Notion fronting service for bounded search, database query, and preparation of page or comment changes.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see only actor-visible summaries within allowed workspace, page, and database scope. Must not receive raw workspace export, hidden page access, or private page exfiltration.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review prepared page creation, page update, and comment previews and decide whether the requested mutation may proceed.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Holds approval authority for preview-only mutation flows, but the exact organizational role and whether authority differs by action type are not specified in the source.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business authority who reviews prepared page creations, page updates, and comments before any mutation is allowed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Should see the prepared preview, relevant bounded context, and approval record needed to make a decision, without expanding access into raw workspace export or hidden content.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review whether bounded access, allowlists, approval records, and denial behavior are operating as intended within the fronting service.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: May or may not be the same person as the mutation approver; the source confirms audit and approval records exist but does not define whether this actor can directly approve changes.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Oversight actor responsible for reviewing audit and approval records for the centralized governed fronting service.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Should see approval records, audit evidence, and scope enforcement behavior relevant to governance review, but not gain unrestricted workspace content access by default.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: governed_notion_requester bounded access to Workspace Context Search should return bounded_result. Allow bounded workspace search within declared workspace scope and return actor-visible summaries only, not raw workspace tools or exports. Matches the governed search capability and read-only bounded result posture.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded workspace search within declared workspace scope and return actor-visible summaries only, not raw workspace tools or exports.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: governed_notion_requester bounded access to Database Context Query should return bounded_result. Allow bounded database query against an explicit database and return summarized context rather than raw export. Filters, sorting, and backend options remain bounded by scope and policy.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow bounded database query against an explicit database and return summarized context rather than raw export.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: governed_notion_requester approval_required access to Page Creation Preparation should return approval_stop. Allow preparation of a page creation preview under an explicit parent, then stop for approval before creation. No direct page creation is allowed from the governed capability surface.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of a page creation preview under an explicit parent, then stop for approval before creation.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: governed_notion_requester approval_required access to Page Update Preparation should return approval_stop. Allow preparation of a page update preview for an explicit page, then stop for approval before applying changes. No direct page update is allowed from the governed capability surface.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of a page update preview for an explicit page, then stop for approval before applying changes.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: governed_notion_requester approval_required access to Comment Preparation should return approval_stop. Allow preparation of a page comment preview from supplied context, then stop for approval before posting. No direct comment posting is allowed from the governed capability surface.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow preparation of a page comment preview from supplied context, then stop for approval before posting.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: governed_notion_requester restricted access to Clarification Handling should return clarification_required. Stop and request clarification when workspace scope, query, page id, parent id, or database id is missing or ambiguous. Applies across search, query, and preparation flows.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Stop and request clarification when workspace scope, query, page id, parent id, or database id is missing or ambiguous.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: governed_notion_requester denied access to Scope and Visibility Controls should return deny_request. Deny requests for hidden page access, private page exfiltration, raw workspace export, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, or workspace-wide unrestricted access. Preserves actor-visible scope and bounded provider controls.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Deny requests for hidden page access, private page exfiltration, raw workspace export, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, or workspace-wide unrestricted access.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: governed_notion_requester denied access to Approval-Gated Changes should return deny_request. Deny direct mutation attempts when approval has not been granted for page creation, page update, or comment posting. Separates preview preparation from actual mutation authority.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Deny direct mutation attempts when approval has not been granted for page creation, page update, or comment posting.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: notion_change_approver allowed access to Approval-Gated Changes should return direct_result. Allow review and approval decision handling for prepared page creation, page update, and comment previews. This rule assumes the approver actor is intended to participate directly in approval flow.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow review and approval decision handling for prepared page creation, page update, and comment previews.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: notion_governance_reviewer allowed access to Policy Enforcement and Audit should return direct_result. Allow governance review of approval records, scope enforcement posture, and audit-relevant outcomes for the governed Notion fronting service. This rule assumes governance review and audit visibility are intended for this actor.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Allow governance review of approval records, scope enforcement posture, and audit-relevant outcomes for the governed Notion fronting service.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read operations can proceed within bounded visible scope. Any page creation, page update, or comment action is preview-only and approval-gated before mutation, with approval records and audit expected as part of the service posture.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product exposes only bounded Notion capabilities. Reads are limited to actor-visible workspace, page, database, and result scope and return summaries rather than raw exports. Missing required context returns clarification-required. Page creation, page updates, and comments stop at preview and require approval before any mutation. Private page exfiltration, hidden page access, raw export, arbitrary block mutation, workspace administration, and unapproved mutations are denied.

required_anip_support: Enforce explicit denial behavior for out-of-policy requests.
Keep the agent-facing surface narrower than the backend capability set.
Make safe-stop outcomes visible and reviewable.
required_behaviors: Private page exfiltration and hidden page access are denied.
Raw workspace export and workspace administration are denied.
Arbitrary block mutation is not available through backend options.
Unapproved mutations do not proceed through the governed interface.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Deny requests for raw export, hidden pages, or unapproved mutation

required_anip_support: Model clarification-required as a first-class governed outcome.
Prevent unsafe defaults and hidden target inference.
Keep required-input behavior consistent across capabilities.
required_behaviors: The service does not guess missing workspace, page, parent, or database targets.
The response clearly indicates clarification is required.
Only the minimum missing input is requested to continue safely.
Execution does not proceed until required context is supplied.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Stop for clarification when required Notion context is missing

required_anip_support: Represent write-adjacent work as governed preparation rather than direct mutation.
Make the approval stop explicit in the runtime contract.
Preserve parent scope and policy controls during preview generation.
required_behaviors: The requester must identify the parent explicitly.
The service produces a preview-only result, not a created page.
Approval is required before any page creation mutation can occur.
Parent override and hidden page access are not allowed through provider options.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Prepare a new page under an explicit parent and stop for approval

required_anip_support: Separate preparation from execution for write-adjacent page changes.
Enforce explicit page targeting and approval posture.
Prevent hidden or arbitrary content mutation through bounded controls.
required_behaviors: The requester must identify the target page explicitly.
The service returns a preview of the proposed update rather than mutating the page.
Approval is required before any update can be applied.
Arbitrary block mutation is not exposed through the governed interface.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Prepare an update to an existing page and stop before applying it

required_anip_support: Provide a safe comment-preparation surface instead of direct comment posting.
Make approval gating explicit for write-adjacent collaboration actions.
Preserve page scope and bounded context handling.
required_behaviors: The requester must identify the page and provide the purpose and context for the comment.
The service prepares a preview rather than posting the comment directly.
Approval is required before the comment can be posted.
The context used for comment preparation remains bounded and policy-controlled.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Prepare a page comment from supplied context and stop before posting

required_anip_support: Provide a governed database query surface with explicit inputs.
Prevent unrestricted export and hidden data access.
Shape results into bounded context suitable for agent consumption.
required_behaviors: The requester must identify the target database explicitly.
Optional filters are allowed only as declared bounded inputs.
Returned rows are summarized and actor-visible, not raw export output.
The service enforces result limits and safe provider controls.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Query a bounded database view and receive summarized rows

required_anip_support: Expose a governed search capability instead of raw workspace tooling.
Enforce scope, visibility, and result-limit policy at runtime.
Return explicit bounded results suitable for agent use.
required_behaviors: The requester must provide workspace_scope and query before search runs.
Results are limited to allowed workspace, page, and database boundaries.
The service returns actor-visible summaries rather than raw exports.
The flow remains read-only and does not expose raw Notion API or MCP tools.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Search bounded workspace context without exposing raw Notion access

service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

business_constraints.assistant_drafted_requirements_summary:
Expose only governed Notion capabilities to agents: The product must provide an ANIP fronting service for Notion that exposes only bounded capabilities for workspace search, database query, page creation preparation, page update preparation, and comment preparation. Agents must not receive raw Notion API access, raw workspace tools, or MCP as their execution surface.
Keep read operations scoped and summary-only: Workspace search and database query must be read-only, limited to allowed workspace, page, and database boundaries, and return actor-visible summarized context rather than raw exports. Result limits must be supported, and provider-specific options may be allowed only when they remain bounded and do not bypass scope or approval policy.
Stop page and comment changes at preview before mutation: Page creation, page updates, and comments must be handled as preparation flows that generate previews only. The system must not create pages, apply updates, or post comments as part of these capabilities. Any actual mutation must remain approval-gated beyond the preview step.
Require explicit identifiers and return clarification-required when context is missing: The product must require explicit inputs for governed operations, including workspace scope for workspace search, database ID for database query, parent ID for page creation preparation, page ID for page update preparation and comment preparation, and the relevant query or summary fields for each flow. When required page, database, parent, workspace scope, or query context is missing or ambiguous, the system must return clarification-required rather than guessing.
Deny unsafe or out-of-bound Notion actions: The product must deny requests that would expose private pages, hidden pages, raw workspace export, workspace administration, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, or any unapproved mutation. Backend options may support bounded provider controls such as selected properties, page size, or safe filter fragments, but must not expand access or bypass governance.
Preserve governance visibility through scope, approvals, and audit: The service must operate with actor-visible scope controls, approval records for change flows, and auditability sufficient for governance review. The deployment posture should support centralized enforcement of allowlists, visibility boundaries, and reviewable outcomes for both bounded reads and denied or approval-stopped actions.
